name: Snowshoe Naan
recipeIngredient: 4 cups unbleached hard white or all-purpose flour (4 to 5 cups)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, combine the warm water and yeast. Stir until dissolved.
2. Add whole wheat flour and mix well.
3. Mix in 1 cup of unbleached hard white or all-purpose flour.
4. Mix for one minute to develop gluten.
5. Cover and let stand for 30 minutes to several hours, covered.
6. Sprinkle salt over the dough, then add another cup of flour and mix.
7. Continue adding flour and mixing until no longer sticky.
8. Turn dough out onto a lightly floured surface and knead thoroughly, about ten minutes, until smooth and easy to handle.
9. Clean the bowl, oil lightly, and return the dough to the bowl. Cover with plastic wrap and let rise in a convenient place for 2 to 3 hours, or until doubled in volume.
10. Gently push down the dough and turn out onto a lightly floured surface.
11. Divide the dough into eight equal pieces.
12. Shape each piece into a flat oval shape, approximately 4 inches wide by 8 inches long.
13. Cover disks with plastic wrap and let rise for about 20 minutes.
14. Preheat oven to 450°F (232°C).
15. Place quarry tiles or large baking stone on the lower third rack of the oven, leaving space between tiles and walls for air circulation.
16. After preheating, dip fingertips in cold water.
17. Make tightly spaced indentations all over each dough disk using your fingertips.
18. Stretch the dough gently into a long oval strip by draping it over both hands and pulling apart slowly.
19. Place the stretched bread on heated quarry tiles or stone in the oven.
20. Sprinkle with black onion seeds.
21. Bake for approximately 4 minutes, until golden patches form and crust is browned.
22. Serve warm or at room temperature.
Yield: 8 rippled flatbreads, about 12 inches long and 3 to 5 inches wide, with both soft and crispy textures.
